N. gonorrhoeae Euro-GASP Phenotype Data Use Case 1

Source: R/data.R
eurogasp_pheno_raw.Rd

Minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) data for Neisseria gonorrhoeae isolates from three European Gonococcal Antimicrobial Surveillance Programme (Euro-GASP) genomic surveys (2013, 2018, 2020), from Harris et al. (2018) https://doi.org/10.1016/S1473-3099(18)30225-1, Sánchez-Busó et al. (2022) https://doi.org/10.1016/S2666-5247(22)00044-1, and Golparian et al. (2024) https://doi.org/10.1016/S2666-5247(23)00370-1.

Usage

eurogasp_pheno_raw

Format

eurogasp_pheno_raw A data frame with 5,361 rows and 5 columns:

  • id: Sample identifier (ENA run accession).

  • Azithromycin: MIC value in mg/L for azithromycin (numeric; n = 5,055).

  • Ciprofloxacin: MIC value in mg/L for ciprofloxacin (numeric; n = 5,360).

  • Cefixime: MIC value in mg/L for cefixime (character, to preserve inequality prefixes such as <0.016).

  • Ceftriaxone: MIC value in mg/L for ceftriaxone (numeric; n = 5,361).

Source

ENA projects PRJEB9227, PRJEB34068, PRJEB58139.

On this page

Free and Open Source (GPL-3), brought by AMRverse/AMRgen Development Team

AMRverse is an ecosystem for AMR data science. We aim to make common tasks easier and faster, via interoperable tools and resources.
